script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'race-followups',
  description: 'Fix 2 rename stragglers (codex-gated) + attempt champion-recipe A/B or record blocked (codex-gated)',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Stragglers' },
    { title: 'AB-Test' },
  ],
}

const STRAG_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  additionalProperties: false,
  required: ['diff', 'verify_ok', 'codex_verdict', 'commit_sha', 'pushed', 'notes'],
  properties: {
    diff: { type: 'string', description: 'the git diff of the straggler fix' },
    verify_ok: { type: 'boolean' },
    codex_verdict: { type: 'string', description: 'codex gate token + one line' },
    commit_sha: { type: 'string' },
    pushed: { type: 'boolean' },
    notes: { type: 'string' },
  },
}

const AB_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  additionalProperties: false,
  required: ['browser_fixed', 'ran_paid_ab', 'baseline_score', 'renamed_score', 'delta', 'blocked_reason', 'codex_verdict', 'cost_usd', 'recipe', 'notes'],
  properties: {
    browser_fixed: { type: 'boolean' },
    ran_paid_ab: { type: 'boolean' },
    baseline_score: { type: 'string' },
    renamed_score: { type: 'string' },
    delta: { type: 'string' },
    blocked_reason: { type: 'string' },
    codex_verdict: { type: 'string' },
    cost_usd: { type: 'string' },
    recipe: { type: 'string', description: 'exact reproduction recipe for a working-browser box' },
    notes: { type: 'string' },
  },
}

phase('Stragglers')
const strag = await agent(`Claude-codex workflow DELIVERABLE 1: fix 2 rename stragglers left by the file-rename PR #1394, then codex-gate.

SETUP: create a CLEAN detached worktree at the committed head of the filerename branch (do NOT use the live /home/polaris/wt/phase-file worktree — it has a concurrent bot's uncommitted changes):
  cd /home/polaris/wt/phase0
  SHA=$(git ls-remote origin refs/heads/chore/review-readiness-filerename | cut -f1)
  WT=/tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/strag_wt
  rm -rf "$WT"; git worktree add --detach "$WT" "$SHA"
  cd "$WT"

FIX #1 — scripts/diagnostics/entailment_rotation_behavioral.py: it imports 'benchmark_run_capture' (line 43) but calls 'pathB_capture.set_role_providers(...)' (line 63) and 'pathB_capture.reset_role_providers(...)' (line 68) — pathB_capture is undefined -> NameError. Change those two calls from pathB_capture -> benchmark_run_capture. (Do NOT touch the import; it is already correct.)

FIX #2 — scripts/operational_readiness_preflight.py line 93: '_PATHB_RUNNER = _REPO_ROOT / "src" / "polaris_graph" / "benchmark" / "pathB_runner.py"' points at the OLD filename. Change 'pathB_runner.py' -> 'benchmark_gate_runner.py' (the renamed file). Leave the doc/comment references (lines 26,328,427,454) as-is — those are prose about pathB_runner.gate_around_question, not a live path.

VERIFY: (a) AST-compile both files with /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python -m py_compile; (b) grep to confirm no remaining bare 'pathB_capture.' call and no 'pathB_runner.py' path literal; (c) confirm the new path file exists: src/polaris_graph/benchmark/benchmark_gate_runner.py.

CODEX-GATE (codex cannot read files here — feed it the diff inline): write the 'git diff' to /tmp/strag.diff, then run:
  cd /tmp && printf 'You are codex-sol. Gate this 2-line rename-straggler fix. It changes an undefined pathB_capture.* call to the already-imported benchmark_run_capture, and updates a stale pathB_runner.py path literal to the renamed benchmark_gate_runner.py. No logic change. Confirm behavior-neutral and correct. Emit STRAG-{SAFE|REVISE}.\\n\\nDIFF:\\n' > /tmp/strag_gate.md && cat /tmp/strag.diff >> /tmp/strag_gate.md && timeout 200 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check -c model_reasoning_effort=high - < /tmp/strag_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -15

COMMIT (only if codex STRAG-SAFE): stage ONLY the 2 fixed files explicitly, commit with trailer 'Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>' and 'Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01TLpJSqNfJVSP1UGdtk89nA', then push: git push origin HEAD:refs/heads/chore/review-readiness-filerename. If push rejects (non-fast-forward due to concurrent activity), report that in notes and do NOT force.

Return the diff, verify result, codex verdict token, commit sha, whether pushed, and notes.`, { schema: STRAG_SCHEMA, phase: 'Stragglers', label: 'fix-stragglers' })

phase('AB-Test')
const ab = await agent(`Claude-codex workflow DELIVERABLE 2: attempt the champion-recipe A/B to EMPIRICALLY confirm the renames don't move the RACE score. This is PAID and BROWSER-dependent — be strict about cost and honesty.

CONTEXT: champion recipe = scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py --corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json --rq-drb-task 72, env PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 + GLM 5.2 (all pinned in /workspace/POLARIS/.env), interpreter /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python (torch cu128, drives the GPU). Reranker needs PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_SCORE_CHUNK=16 (byte-identical, avoids OOM). The composer does a LIVE agentic gap-fill that needs a working browser. On this box playwright chrome-headless-shell works via a userspace lib fix (LD_LIBRARY_PATH at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/browserlibs/LDPATH.txt) BUT the CRAWL4AI fetch backend fails with TargetClosedError -> composes produced no report.

STEP 1 — BROWSER: diagnose why CRAWL4AI's chromium fails (it may launch the FULL chromium, not chrome-headless-shell; run ldd on ~/.cache/ms-playwright/chromium-*/chrome-linux/chrome and check for still-missing libs; try adding them to the userspace lib dir like the existing fix, or set CRAWL4AI to headless/no-sandbox). Prove a fix by launching it and fetching one URL. Time-box this to a reasonable effort.

STEP 2 — GATE THE SPEND: only if STEP 1 makes the browser actually work, proceed. If the browser CANNOT be fixed, DO NOT run any paid compose. Set browser_fixed=false, ran_paid_ab=false, and write blocked_reason + the exact recipe for a working-browser box, and STOP (still run the codex gate on this blocked decision).

STEP 3 — A/B (only if browser works): run the champion recipe on TWO trees with IDENTICAL config, RACE-score both in the SAME batch (judge openai/gpt-5.5, task 72):
  - baseline: /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch gate-inversion, pre-rename)
  - renamed: a clean detached worktree off chore/review-readiness-filerename
  Cap: ONE compose per tree (no retries beyond 1). Report baseline_score, renamed_score, delta, and total cost_usd. The rename is EXONERATED if |delta| <= 0.016 (judge variance).

STEP 4 — CODEX-GATE (feed inline, codex can't read files): summarize the outcome (browser fixed? scores? delta? or blocked+reason) into /tmp/ab_gate.md prefixed with 'You are codex-sol. Gate this A/B methodology and conclusion about whether the file renames moved the RACE score. Is the conclusion sound and honestly scoped? Emit AB-{SOUND|REVISE}.' then: cd /tmp && timeout 220 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check -c model_reasoning_effort=high - < /tmp/ab_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -20

Return all schema fields honestly. Do NOT fabricate scores — if no report was produced, scores are 'N/A' and ran_paid_ab=false.`, { schema: AB_SCHEMA, phase: 'AB-Test', label: 'ab-test' })

return { strag, ab }

strag.diff:
diff --git a/scripts/diagnostics/entailment_rotation_behavioral.py b/scripts/diagnostics/entailment_rotation_behavioral.py
index 717895b..9e586f0 100644
--- a/scripts/diagnostics/entailment_rotation_behavioral.py
+++ b/scripts/diagnostics/entailment_rotation_behavioral.py
@@ -60,12 +60,12 @@ def _run(rotate: bool):
     else:
         os.environ.pop("PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE", None)
     # Resolve the mirror provider to the chain LEAD (novita), exactly as a live preflight would.
-    token = pathB_capture.set_role_providers({"mirror": "novita"})
+    token = benchmark_run_capture.set_role_providers({"mirror": "novita"})
     try:
         judge = entailment_judge._EntailmentJudge()
         verdict, reason = judge.judge(SENT, SPAN)
     finally:
-        pathB_capture.reset_role_providers(token)
+        benchmark_run_capture.reset_role_providers(token)
     is_judge_error = isinstance(reason, str) and reason.startswith("judge_error:")
     print(f"  rotate={'ON ' if rotate else 'OFF'} -> verdict={verdict!r} "
           f"judge_error={is_judge_error} reason={reason[:60]!r}", flush=True)
diff --git a/scripts/operational_readiness_preflight.py b/scripts/operational_readiness_preflight.py
index 5462511..5bd38f2 100644
--- a/scripts/operational_readiness_preflight.py
+++ b/scripts/operational_readiness_preflight.py
@@ -90,7 +90,7 @@ if str(_REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
 _DICED_PREFLIGHT = _REPO_ROOT / "scripts" / "pipeline_diced_preflight.py"
 # The honest_sweep --pathB-gate wrapper (read READ-ONLY via AST; never imported) — the launch-path
 # check confirms it does NOT apply the slate, so the paid run must launch via run_gate_b.py instead.
-_PATHB_RUNNER = _REPO_ROOT / "src" / "polaris_graph" / "benchmark" / "pathB_runner.py"
+_PATHB_RUNNER = _REPO_ROOT / "src" / "polaris_graph" / "benchmark" / "benchmark_gate_runner.py"
 
 GREEN = "GREEN"
 RED = "RED"
strag.codex_verdict: STRAG-SAFE — behavior-neutral rename cleanup: both capture calls now use the existing import; preflight path targets the renamed runner. No logic change.
strag.notes:
Clean detached worktree created at committed head f423241 of chore/review-readiness-filerename (NOT the live phase-file worktree). Both fixes applied exactly as specified: FIX #1 changed the two undefined pathB_capture.set_role_providers/reset_role_providers calls (lines 63,68) to benchmark_run_capture (import at line 43 untouched); FIX #2 changed the pathB_runner.py path literal (line 93) to benchmark_gate_runner.py (doc/comment refs at 26,328,427,454 untouched).

VERIFY: (a) py_compile OK for both files. (b) grep confirms NO remaining bare pathB_capture.* CALL and NO pathB_runner.py PATH LITERAL in either fixed file — the residual grep hits are all prose comments in OTHER files (scripts/dr_benchmark/pathB_run_gate.py:702 comment, scripts/run_console/run_console.py:24 doc path, operational_readiness_preflight.py:454 excluded-by-spec doc ref, scripts/architecture/weekly_drift_report.py:169 prose), not live code. (c) Renamed target file src/polaris_graph/benchmark/benchmark_gate_runner.py exists (15520 bytes).

CODEX: STRAG-SAFE at model_reasoning_effort=high (diff fed inline; codex's own file-read attempt failed on bwrap sandbox but it gated purely on the inline diff as intended).

COMMIT: staged only the 2 files explicitly; commit e1f33a8 with both required trailers. PUSH: fast-forward success f423241..e1f33a8 -> chore/review-readiness-filerename (no non-fast-forward rejection, no force needed).

ab.baseline_score: 0.3782 (RACE Overall; Comprehensiveness 0.3892 / Insight 0.3633 / Instruction-Following 0.3797 / Readability 0.3866; judge openai/gpt-5.5, DRB task 72)
ab.blocked_reason: A/B INCONCLUSIVE on the score axis, NOT browser-blocked. The browser was fixed and BOTH paid composes ran, but the renamed-tree compose crashed before producing any report, so there is no renamed_score and no delta. Crash: at the FIRST outline-generation LLM call (_call_outline), the reasoning-first code model deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro returned finish_reason='length' (hit its 32768 output-token cap: 32768 reasoning tokens, empty content, 232s call); the pipeline's SF-15 fail-loud guard correctly refused to promote truncated scratchpad as verified prose (ReasoningFirstTruncationError). This is consistent with stochastic model-side truncation: the identical first call on baseline used a byte-identical 24567-token input prompt and finished cleanly (7040 out/6412 reasoning tokens, 38s). No evidence links the crash to the file renames, but per codex-gate scoping this run CANNOT rule out a branch-induced contribution NOR exonerate the branch on RACE. The |delta|<=0.016 exoneration test could not be applied. Operator cap = ONE compose per tree (no retries beyond 1); the one renamed compose was spent on the crashed run and was not retried. SCOPE DISCOVERY: branch chore/review-readiness-filerename is NOT a pure file-rename — it also refactored env reads (os.getenv -> resolve()/get_model_settings()) and renamed modules (e.g. pathB_capture -> benchmark_run_capture); however both trees resolved identical models (agent z-ai/glm-5.2, code deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro) and the crashing call's input token count was identical.
ab.codex_verdict: AB-REVISE — codex agreed the A/B is honestly INCONCLUSIVE (delta=N/A; branch neither exonerated nor incriminated under |delta|<=0.016), but flagged my draft claim 'the crash was NOT rename-caused' as too strong: equal input-token counts and resolved model names do not prove byte-identical serialized requests nor exclude branch refactor effects. Adopted framing: 'The failure is consistent with stochastic model-side truncation, and no evidence links it to the file renames. Because the branch contains additional refactors and produced no score, this run cannot rule out a branch-induced contribution or exonerate the branch on RACE.'
ab.cost_usd: ~5.27 (baseline compose $4.91 from pg_cost_ledger.jsonl + renamed failed compose $0.16 from renamed-tree ledger + RACE gpt-5.5 judge ~$0.20 est, not ledgered by the DRB harness)
ab.recipe: BROWSER FIX (proven): crawl4ai launches the FULL playwright chromium at ~/.cache/ms-playwright/chromium-1223/chrome-linux64/chrome (NOT headless_shell), which needs libcairo.so.2 + libpango-1.0.so.0 plus transitive deps. On a box lacking them: apt-get download libcairo2 libpango-1.0-0 libpangocairo-1.0-0 libpangoft2-1.0-0 libharfbuzz0b libfribidi0 libthai0 libthai-data libdatrie1 libpixman-1-0 libfontconfig1 libfreetype6 libgraphite2-3 libxrender1 libxcb-render0 libxcb-shm0 ; dpkg-deb -x each into the userspace root/ tree already on LD_LIBRARY_PATH (scratchpad/browserlibs/root); verify with `ldd chrome-linux64/chrome | grep 'not found'` = empty. Prove: crawl4ai AsyncWebCrawler(headless=True, extra_args=['--no-sandbox','--disable-dev-shm-usage']).arun('https://example.com') from a NON-project cwd (avoid an 'inspect' shadow) with unset PYTHONPATH -> success/status 200. COMPOSE (per tree): export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=$(cat scratchpad/browserlibs/LDPATH.txt); export PG_LOOPBACK_MODE=0 (CRITICAL: .env pins PG_LOOPBACK_MODE=1 which hangs forever with no responder; shell wins since dotenv loads override=False); export PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_SCORE_CHUNK=16; export OPENROUTER_API_KEY via `python -c "from dotenv import dotenv_values; print(dotenv_values('.env')['OPENROUTER_API_KEY'])"` (do NOT bash-source .env — line 304 'PG_EXA_CATEGORY=research paper' breaks bash; the guard at composer line 193 reads shell env BEFORE dotenv runs); unset PYTHONPATH; cd TREE; /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py --corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json --rq-drb-task 72. Renamed worktree needs symlinks: .env -> /workspace/POLARIS/.env, data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json and third_party/deep_research_bench -> baseline tree (gitignored). SCORE both report.md in the same batch: /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python scripts/score_report_race.py --report <run_dir>/report.md --task-id 72 --model-name <label> --race-model openai/gpt-5.5 (run from the baseline tree which holds DRB data; needs OPENROUTER_API_KEY). RISK: the reasoning-first code model deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro can stochastically truncate at 32768 output tokens on the first outline call and fail the whole compose (SF-15 fail-loud) — to make the A/B robust on a working-browser box, raise the outliner code-model max_tokens budget or allow >1 compose retry, otherwise one tree can yield no score as happened here.
ab.notes: STEP 1 browser: FIXED + double-proven (isolated example.com fetch HTTP 200; and live in the baseline champion compose 'CRAWL4AI: Succeeded for jpmorgan.com (10182 chars, status 200)', 29/30 live sources fetched). STEP 2 gate: browser worked, so proceeding to paid A/B was authorized. STEP 3 A/B: config made byte-identical across trees (corpus md5 identical, .env shared, composer script diff -q IDENTICAL, both resolved agent=z-ai/glm-5.2 code=deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro, both import-clean via polaris_graph.graph smoke test). BASELINE succeeded: cp4_used=agentic, degraded_to_seed=false, real 9-section/2926-word span-grounded report, RACE Overall 0.3782. RENAMED crashed at first outline call on stochastic deepseek truncation before writing any report (its out_dir agentic_report_20260720_014742 is empty) -> no renamed score. First paid renamed attempt (before I fixed the API-key export) exited $0 at the OPENROUTER_API_KEY guard with zero LLM calls, so it did NOT consume the compose budget; the one real renamed compose is the crashed run. No retry per the cap. Honest bottom line: browser exoneration COMPLETE; rename score-exoneration NOT achieved (inconclusive). Artifacts: baseline report /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/outputs/agentic_report_20260720_005837/report.md; RACE result .../third_party/deep_research_bench/results/race/polaris_ab_baseline/race_result.txt; logs in scratchpad/{baseline.log,renamed.log,race_baseline.log}; codex gate /tmp/ab_gate.md. Recommend the operator re-run the renamed compose once (or bump deepseek outliner max_tokens) to obtain renamed_score and actually apply the |delta|<=0.016 test.

summary: Fix 2 rename stragglers (codex-gated) + attempt champion-recipe A/B or record blocked (codex-gated)

workflowProgress.label: fix-stragglers
workflowProgress.promptPreview:

SETUP: create a CLEAN detached worktree at the committed head of the filerename branch (do NOT use the live /home/polaris/wt/phase-file worktree — it has a concurrent bot's uncommitted changes):
  cd /home/polaris/wt/phase0
  SHA=$(git ls-remote origin refs/heads/chore/review-readiness-…

workflowProgress.promptPreview:
